title: Bridge Essay: A New Perspective on Predictive Motor Signaling.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nested agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.
**Phase context:** Sense of agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.

**A New Perspective on Predictive Motor Signaling.** — Curr Biol (2018). DOI: 10.1016/j.cub.2018.01.033. PMID: 29510116. Adaptive behavior relies on complex neural processing in multiple interacting networks of both motor and sensory systems. One such interaction employs intrinsic neuronal signals, so-called 'corollary discharge' or 'efference copy', that may be used to predict the sensory consequences of a specific behavioral action, thereby enabling self-generated (reafferent) sensory information and extrinsic (exafferent) sensory inflow to be dissociated.
